commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: - [P1] This draft needs maintainer product/security review and contributor live proof; the remaining action is not a safe automated repair lane.; Needs attention: The patch introduces a credential-sensitive in-process LLM bridge but does not yet fully protect env credentials or transient transcript data.; Review finding: [P1] Strip gateway credentials from the embedded env — extensions/lobster/src/lobster-runner.ts:... (sha=63709007a0143ecc3f81f5d7f3be47bfe3d53a66)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a Lobster embedded-runner bridge that passes host-provided `llmAdapters` into the runtime and implements an `openclaw` in-process LLM adapter backed by `api.runtime.agent.runEmbeddedAgent`, with one runner unit test.

PR surface: Source +133, Tests +42. Total +175 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the review findings: source inspection shows `ctx.env` still copies `process.env`, the dependency passes provider separately from `payload.model`, and the adapter creates unmanaged temp session files. I did not run a live embedded Lobster E2E.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] This draft needs maintainer product/security review and contributor live proof; the remaining action is not a safe automated repair lane.

target.title: feat(lobster): in-process LLM adapters for embedded runner (#90909)
target.body:
## Summary

Implements the embedded-runner LLM bridge proposed in #90909 (follow-up to #76101, which was closed docs-only by #78736).

`openclaw.invoke` is HTTP-only and would require injecting operator credentials into the workflow environment to work in the embedded runner — the security exposure that was (correctly) declined. Instead, this wires up the engine's **native in-process path**: it populates the embedded runner's `ctx.llmAdapters` with an in-process adapter built from `api.runtime.agent.runEmbeddedAgent` (the same entry `extensions/llm-task` already uses), so embedded workflows can run `llm.invoke --provider openclaw` with **no gateway URL and no token**.

## What problem does this solve?

Embedded (in-process) Lobster can't run LLM steps today. The documented `openclaw.invoke --tool llm-task` pattern fails in the bundled runner because `openclaw.invoke` always needs `OPENCLAW_URL` + a bearer token (it `fetch()`es the gateway — see `@clawdbot/lobster` `openclaw_invoke.js`). The engine's `llm.invoke` command already supports an in-process path via `ctx.llmAdapters` / `ctx.registry`, but `createEmbeddedToolContext` never populated those fields.

## Why it matters / security

Unblocks deterministic LLM orchestration inside embedded Lobster **without** the credential exposure of the rejected approach. No gateway URL or token is placed in `ctx.env`, so workflow shell steps cannot read or exfiltrate operator access. A unit test asserts this directly.

## What changed (self-contained in `extensions/lobster/src/`, +186/-2)

- `lobster-runner.ts`: add `EmbeddedLlmAdapter` / `EmbeddedRuntimeBridges` types; `createEmbeddedToolContext` + `createEmbeddedLobsterRunner` accept host-provided bridges and set `ctx.llmAdapters` / `ctx.registry`. No credentials are injected into `ctx.env`.
- `lobster-tool.ts`: `createLobsterTool(api)` builds an in-process `openclaw` LLM adapter (maps the `llm.invoke` payload ⇄ response envelope via `runEmbeddedAgent`) and passes it down as bridges.
- `lobster-runner.test.ts`: new test — `ctx.llmAdapters` is populated AND `ctx.env` contains no `OPENCLAW_URL` / `OPENCLAW_TOKEN` / `CLAWD_URL` / `CLAWD_TOKEN`.

## Change type
- [x] Feature

## Real behavior proof (honest status — this is a Draft)

Verified locally on a fresh `main` checkout:
- `tsc --noEmit -p extensions/lobster/tsconfig.json` → the two changed source files add **no new type errors** (only 3 pre-existing, unrelated `lobster-taskflow.ts` errors remain on the baseline).
- `vitest run extensions/lobster/src/lobster-runner.test.ts` → **18/18 tests pass**, including the new security test.

Not yet verified here (needs CI / a configured env with LLM credentials): a live end-to-end `llm.invoke --provider openclaw` actually invoking a model inside the embedded runner. Marking this Draft until that proof is produced.

## Out of scope / open to maintainer decision

Per #90909, the exact adapter contract, the provider name (`openclaw` vs a distinct `embedded`), and the relationship to the broader nested `/tools/invoke` work are design calls — happy to adjust to your preference. The `artifacts`→prompt mapping and provider/model resolution mirror `llm-task` and are open to refinement.

## Linked issue

Implements #90909. Refs #76101.
target.author: bosszukung
